summary:
Septoplasty corrects nasal septum deviations but does not address or reverse the biomechanical causes leading to compensatory hypertrophy of the middle turbinate. Thus, it does not significantly alter the dimensions of this structural adaptation.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{No}}$

The summary focuses only on clinically significant facts related to whether septoplasty affects compensatory hypertrophy of the middle turbinate—specifically emphasizing its lack of impact due to no alteration of the underlying aerodynamic forces causing such growth.
